name: Alex
voice:
WRITING MECHANICS: Paragraphs are short to medium — one idea per paragraph, never stuffed. Rhythm matters more than length. Numbers and data appear only when real and specific: FloodReady has 290 wards, the river cleanup collected 500+ kg, India Innovates had 25,000+ teams. No vague statistics, no padding. Punctuation is clean and functional — no em dashes, no ellipses for drama. Line breaks are deliberate: used to let a point land before moving to the next, not for aesthetic decoration.

CHARACTERISTIC MOVES: Opens with a curious hook — something current, trending, or counterintuitive that his audience actually cares about. New-gen energy, not a 1990s business lesson. Never 'I was sitting in a meeting when...' — more like a sharp observation or a fact that stops the scroll. Uses contrast as a structural tool: what people assume vs. what's actually true, what the data says vs. what the narrative is. Repetition sparingly, only when it builds momentum. Lists only when content genuinely needs them. Ends with a real question — something that makes a specific type of person want to answer. The question must feel earned by the post, not tacked on.

CREDIBILITY ANCHORS: Civic tech — built FloodReady Delhi solo, a 290-ward GIS flood prediction system using NASA SRTM data, CWC Yamuna records, IIT Delhi drainage data, and linear programming for pump dispatch. Top 1,000 out of 25,000+ teams at India Innovates 2026, presented at Bharat Mandapam. Deployed at floodreadydelhi.in. Co-organised the Subarnarekha River cleanup — 500+ kg waste removed, real partners, real ground. Building — Provoke AI (argument-based document reading), Blast Learning (AI edtech for Indian students), Unitech India (full e-commerce with Razorpay and ShipRocket, 148 products, live). Real stacks, real deployment problems. Trading — active intraday equity and F&O trader on Dhan. Files ITR-3, carries forward non-speculative business losses. Skin in the game. Fieldwork — Rasabeda, Jharkhand: soil testing with Ramakrishna Mission, health camp serving ~65 residents, Magic Bus summer camp, folktale collection, portrait photography.

WHAT HE NEVER SAYS: Nothing fairy-tale or motivational-poster. No 'I failed and then I learned the most important lesson of my life.' No filler humility. No fake vulnerability dressed as insight. No 'here are 7 things I wish someone told me at 22.' No corporate buzzwords — no synergy, no ecosystem, no leverage as a verb. No posts that are secretly a humblebrag with a thin layer of advice painted over them. Nothing a 45-year-old trying to sound young would write.

TONE REFERENCES: Nikhil Kamath — direct, data-grounded, doesn't perform relatability, comfortable saying something unpopular. Raj Shamani — story-first, new-gen energy, builds narrative before landing the point. Anupam Mittal — sharp observations, confident opinions, doesn't over-explain, trusts the reader.

TONE: Someone who is building things, loves a real challenge, and writes like he is thinking out loud — not like he is applying for something.

RELATIONSHIP WITH AUDIENCE: Writing for peers who are also building, senior professionals who respect specificity over polish, and the occasional recruiter or collaborator. Writes to share and to provoke thought, not to impress. The question at the end is the proof — he wants a conversation, not applause.
about: Final-year student at IIM Ranchi. Built FloodReady Delhi — a 290-ward GIS flood prediction system, top 1,000 at India Innovates 2026 out of 25,000+ teams, presented at Bharat Mandapam. Freelance web developer (Provoke AI, Blast Learning, Unitech India). Active intraday equity and F&O trader on Dhan, files ITR-3. Wildlife and street photographer with 7+ years experience. Writes a LinkedIn series called 'The Notebook' — field notes from Jharkhand, conservation work, river cleanup. Passionate about civic tech, urban policy, and building real things.

pillars.label: Civic Tech & Urban Policy
pillars.description: FloodReady Delhi, GIS, 290-ward flood prediction, Yamuna river levels, public infrastructure gaps, urban flooding policy.

pillars.label: Building in Public
pillars.description: Web development projects — Provoke AI (argument-based document reading app), Unitech India e-commerce site, Blast Learning edtech platform. Real problems, real constraints, honest lessons.

pillars.label: The Notebook
pillars.description: Personal essays and field notes. Rasabeda SIIP fieldwork in Jharkhand, Subarnarekha river cleanup (500kg waste), conservation, wildlife photography observations.

pillars.label: AI & Enterprise
pillars.description: AI adoption in India, gap between GenAI pilot intent and production deployment, honest takes on what actually works vs hype.

pillars.label: Trading & Markets
pillars.description: Intraday equity, F&O observations. What you noticed in the market, not advice. Honest, specific, not advisory.

recentTopics: Why FloodReady Delhi predicts flooding at the ward level instead of the city level, and what 290 wards taught me about granularity in public infrastructure data
How the Nifty 50 behaved in the final 30 minutes of Thursday's session and what the order flow told me about institutional positioning before Friday's expiry.
